Guys, one of the earlist patches mentioned above by me fixes the issue totally for taking photos in all Pixel Android 13 devices:

```
dependency_overrides:
  camera_android:
    git:
      url: https://github.com/elmar001/plugins.git
      path: packages/camera/camera_android
      ref: 5172b44088a4082ff0899594614c5380457d341b
```

It is tested and currently in use in my producation app. There may be an issue with recording a video only which mentioned by some other developers. If it does not work, you most probably did not add the dependecy correctly and it is not overriding camera package.
Can you please stop spamming? We are all aware of the issues and we subscribed to see actual updates. You can ask your questions in other platforms.

Just pass the compileSdkVersion 33 to 32, so, We force use API Android 12 in Android 13.
It's working for me.

camera: ^0.10.0+4

> Just pass the compileSdkVersion 33 to 32, so, We force use API Android 12 in Android 13. It's working for me.
> 
> camera: ^0.10.0+4

It's not a proper fix.
I will report the issue with the TQ1A* builds. Just to reiterate, the fix is in those builds so, TP* builds are still expected to fail.

I debugged the issue and it seems it is coming from the Android 13 OS. Either way, @googlebot needs to update documentation for solving the issue, or we need to find any other way to use the camera package.

> I will report the issue with the TQ1A* builds. Just to reiterate, the fix is in those builds so, TP* builds are still expected to fail.

any update for this issue?
All you clownin dawgs asking for updates need to learn some patience or solve it yourselves and post back here with a solution better than the workaround.

> > I will report the issue with the TQ1A* builds. Just to reiterate, the fix is in those builds so, TP* builds are still expected to fail.
> 
> @camsim99: what does this mean? how can I be sure I'm using a TQ1A* build? which library is this? please clarify.

The problem is with Android 13. The latest TQ1A* build fixed this issue. Check the build number on your device for details.
@ianfmc You can check the build number by following the steps in this article: https://support.google.com/android/answer/7680439?hl=en.
There is a PR under review to workaround this bug from @MbIXjkee in the meantime, as well: https://github.com/flutter/plugins/pull/6867

> @acoutts
> 
> Thanks for the quick fix, camera works and we can see the camera but when we start recording video (`startVideoRecording`)it gets this error: (but taking picture works fine)
> 
> ```
> E/MethodChannel#plugins.flutter.io/camera_android( 6270): Failed to handle method call
> E/MethodChannel#plugins.flutter.io/camera_android( 6270): java.lang.NullPointerException: Attempt to invoke virtual method 'java.util.List android.media.EncoderProfiles.getVideoProfiles()' on a null object reference
> ```

Here is a quick fix for both Camera and Video recording with the help of @acoutts and @elmar001 PRs

```yaml
dependency_overrides:
  camera_android:
    git:
      url: https://github.com/OmarHatem28/plugins.git
      path: packages/camera/camera_android
      ref: 8550e0e698f3d358f42d60b090320dea7c3c0775
```

It uses the legacy functions for both camera and video in only targetSdkVersion 33

for some reason, the PR under review by @MbIXjkee didn't work with video recording and still threw the above exception although the file changes seem logical

@josh88versa With the release of 3.7, there are a few issues and regressions that have appeared. While the team works on those, they will eventually get to this issue. There is still time until Play Store required SDK target 33 (I believe we have time till August of this year), so for now, you can try the multiple workarounds in this thread while the team works on it. Rest be assured, they are very aware of this issue, and are probably discussing, or even implementing a fix as we speak :) As I understand from the conversation of this bug, it's not a simple one to fix as there have been some attempts and while it works for some, it doesn't work for others

@elmar001 To which versions of the camera package does your fix in camera_android apply to?
And what about other devices than Google Pixel (e.g. Samsung Galaxy) which also run Android 13 meanwhile?

To update this issue, I started working on a PR to fix this issue https://github.com/flutter/plugins/pull/7073, based on the discussion and https://github.com/flutter/plugins/pull/6867.

> @elmar001 To which versions of the camera package does your fix in camera_android apply to? And what about other devices than Google Pixel (e.g. Samsung Galaxy) which also run Android 13 meanwhile?

My solution fixes issue on all Android 13s for taking photo. Myself tested prod app with this fix in Galaxy A13 Android 13 and Google Pixel 4A Android 13. Some other people reported here above that capturing video may still have problem. My app does not use video capturing so I am unaware about that.
@elmar001 Thank you. But i meant the version(s) of the camera package itself. Your fix is from October 10, this means version 0.10.0+3. But what about e.g. the current version 0.10.3?

The issue is still not resolved in 0.10.3. 
Simply try running the example code of this plugin with compileSDK 33. The steps are reproduced.
Patching it by compiling through SDK 31 is not an adequate solution as many plugins now require SDK 33.
Request to escalate this issue to priority.

@PerLycke why havent they updated https://pub.dev/packages/camera package

> @PerLycke why havent they updated https://pub.dev/packages/camera package

Because it's not needed. It's a transitive dependency which got an incremental update and will be updated if you run the upgrade command.
I confirm upgrading `camera_android` to 0.10.4 works even after reverting `targetSdkVersion` to 33 :+1:

> I confirm upgrading `camera_android` to 0.10.4 works even after reverting `targetSdkVersion` to 33 :+1: 

What's your minSDK version?

> 
> What's your minSDK version?

21 but it shouldn't be relevant.
@mauriziopinotti when 0.10.4 release??

> @mauriziopinotti when 0.10.4 release??

Version 0.10.4 of [camera_android](https://pub.dev/packages/camera_android/score) has been released (not to be confused with [camera](https://pub.dev/packages/camera)).
Why is this issue closed? The [camera](https://pub.dev/packages/camera) plugin still doesn't work, I don't want to use [camera_android](https://pub.dev/packages/camera_android/score).

> Why is this issue closed? The [camera](https://pub.dev/packages/camera) plugin still doesn't work, I don't want to use [camera_android](https://pub.dev/packages/camera_android/score).

You don't need to use camera_android explicitly, it's already used by camera package, because it's a transitive dependency. All you need to do is to run `flutter pub upgrade` and it will automatically update dependencies, including camera_android. And then you will see that this issue has been fixed.

> > Why is this issue closed? The [camera](https://pub.dev/packages/camera) plugin still doesn't work, I don't want to use [camera_android](https://pub.dev/packages/camera_android/score).
> 
> You don't need to use camera_android explicitly, it's already used by camera package, because it's a transitive dependency. All you need to do is to run `flutter pub upgrade` and it will automatically update dependencies, including camera_android. And then you will see that this issue has been fixed.

> > You could try

> >   camera_android: 0.10.4
> > ```

> Not working，I don't know if it only works on application instead of plugin, I've try to clean and rebuild, but still not working, should I file a new issue for tracking?

Just do a pub upgrade, camera_android is a transitive dependency. After upgrading your pubspec.lock should look like this:

```
  camera_android:
    dependency: transitive
    description:
      name: camera_android
      sha256: e491c836147f60dd8a54cf3895fd2960e13b21b78a9d15b563a1b6c70894f142
      url: "https://pub.dev"
    source: hosted
    version: "0.10.4"
```

## ☂️ Bring Material 3 to Flutter

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6655696/189097522-4efa697c-304b-4c10-948e-fc4cf2fb6758.png)

[**Material 3**](https://m3.material.io), also known as "Material You", is the next generation of Material Design. The major changes include:
- updates to many components (colors, text styles and shapes, etc.)
- color system improvements
- typography improvements
- elevation refinements
 
This issue has tracked the progress and status of adding Material 3 support to the Flutter `material` library. That work is now complete.

## As of Flutter 3.16, Material3 is the default

Support for Material 2 will continue to be available for at least a year after Material 3 becomes the default. To opt-in to Material 2 use the [`useMaterial3`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/ThemeData/useMaterial3.html) `ThemeData` flag in your app:

```dart
  return MaterialApp(
    theme: ThemeData(useMaterial3: false),
    // ...
  );
```
> **Note**
> See the API docs for [`useMaterial3`] for the comprehensive list of changes.

# Updates available in [Flutter 3.16](https://docs.flutter.dev/release/whats-new#15-november-2023-316-release):

Check out the updates to components, color, typography, and elevation in the [Material 3 sample app](https://flutter.github.io/samples/material_3.html).

[![image](https://github-production-user-asset-6210df.s3.amazonaws.com/1377460/237530594-5f002eee-67cd-4ea9-8dc5-4c103f0cdc39.png)](https://flutter.github.io/samples/web/material_3_demo/#/)


## Components
The following components have been updated to Material 3 colors, text styles and shapes generated from the [Material 3 token](https://m3.material.io/foundations/design-tokens/overview) database:
